methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron. The nuclear energy sector comprises a peer universe of 11 companies—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private peers, but filtering for same-stage pre-seed companies, adjacent-stage cohorts, or similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than 3 datapoints in each category. A median derived from the full sector peer set would conflate disparate stages and business maturity levels, introducing material bias. This scarcity of narrow-stage comparables is characteristic of early-stage nuclear tech, where most funded entrants operate in Series A and later rounds.

Without sufficient granular peer data, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range using comparable company methods. Proceeding would require either (1) expansion of the comp set to include less-aligned nuclear and clean-tech peers with acknowledged methodological trade-offs, or (2) reliance on alternative methods such as venture capital method modeling or expert-derived stage priors. The primary uncertainty is the absence of pre-seed nuclear energy transactions with disclosed terms. Subsequent rounds of fundraising for Deep Atomic will generate market evidence to calibrate valuation against the existing private peer set.
